concerns.text: FR-001 mandates ingesting raw corrosion datasets from Zenodo. The plan (research.md) explicitly states 'No verified corrosion dataset is available' and proposes a 'Synthetic Corrosion Dataset' instead. The plan lacks a concrete task to locate, verify, and ingest a real Zenodo dataset, leaving FR-001 unbacked in the implementation path.

SC-005 requires measuring dataset coverage against source Zenodo datasets with ≥70% retention and ≥200 valid records. The plan relies on a synthetic generator for testing. There is no plan element to measure retention rates against a real source dataset, making SC-005 untestable in the current plan.

SC-003 requires measuring SHAP feature importance against a 'Reference Importance Vector' derived from 5 specific metallurgical review papers. The plan mentions SHAP analysis but does not include a task to identify, retrieve, and construct this Reference Vector, leaving the success criterion unmeasured.

FR-007 requires splitting the dataset into training and an OOD test set based on alloy class (e.g., holding out high-entropy alloys). The plan's data strategy relies on synthetic data generation. There is no explicit task to define alloy classes in the synthetic generator to ensure an OOD split is possible, risking FR-007 failure.

FR-009 mandates flagging missing environmental variables and widening confidence intervals by [deferred]. While the plan acknowledges missing variables, it does not explicitly include a task to implement the 'widening' logic or the specific [deferred] marker in the output metrics, leaving the requirement partially unbacked.
